fields.label: Automation Trigger
fields.description: The automation engine this row configures. Read-only. Open the row (click the arrow) to see exactly what it does, how it affects your data, and why it exists.

fields.label: Key
fields.description: Internal immutable identity that links this row to the engine and its Frappe Scheduled Job Type. Never edit.

fields.label: Enabled
fields.description: ON = the engine runs automatically on its schedule (and the matching Scheduled Job Type is active). OFF = it never fires on a timer (you can still use its Run Now button). Saving applies this immediately.

fields.label: Frequency
fields.description: How often the engine runs. Daily = every day at the Time below. Weekly = once a week on the Weekday below, at the Time. Cron = use the advanced Cron Override field instead.

fields.label: Time (HH:MM)
fields.description: 24-hour HH:MM, e.g. 07:30. This is exactly when the engine fires (server timezone). Editing it rewrites the underlying Frappe Scheduled Job Type immediately - no restart or migrate needed. (Also shown in the engine overview panel above the table.)

fields.label: Weekday
fields.options:
Sunday
Monday
Tuesday
Wednesday
Thursday
Friday
Saturday
fields.description: Used only when Frequency = Weekly. The day of the week the engine runs (e.g. Weekly Dynamic Buffer Management runs Sunday).

fields.label: Cron Override (advanced)
fields.description: Advanced: a raw 5-field cron string (minute hour day month weekday), e.g. '*/15 * * * *'. When filled it WINS over Frequency/Time/Weekday. Leave blank for normal use. Invalid crons are rejected on save.

fields.label: What / Effect / Why
fields.description: Plain-English explanation of what this engine does, how it changes your data, and why it exists. Auto-filled - read-only.

fields.label: Pending Voucher Statuses (override; leave blank to use the global TOC Settings defaults)
Pending SO Statuses (Status : Workflow State)
fields.description: Multiselect of Status:WorkflowState pairs this engine treats as still-pending Sales Order demand — same picker as the global 'Pending Sales Order Statuses'. Blank = inherit the global TOC Settings list. Applies only to engines that read SOs (see 'Reads Sales Orders' below).

fields.label: Pending WO Statuses (Status : Workflow State)
fields.description: Multiselect of Status:WorkflowState pairs this engine treats as in-progress Work Order supply — same picker as the global 'Pending Work Order Statuses'. Blank = inherit the global TOC Settings list. Applies only to engines that read WOs.

fields.label: Pending PO Statuses (Status : Workflow State)
fields.description: Multiselect of Status:WorkflowState pairs this engine treats as incoming Purchase Order supply — same picker as the global 'Pending Purchase Order Statuses'. Blank = inherit the global TOC Settings list. Applies only to engines that read POs.

fields.label: Pending Purchase MR Statuses
fields.description: Multiselect of Material Request statuses (purpose = Purchase) this engine treats as in-flight purchase supply. The engine creates Draft Purchase MRs for purchase-mode items and nets their pending qty out of the shortage so it never re-requests the same shortage forever (a PO can't be auto-created — the supplier is your choice). Blank = inherit the built-in default (Draft, Pending, Partially Ordered). Applies only to engines that create Purchase MRs.

fields.label: Applies To (read-only) - which pending lists this engine actually reads
Reads Sales Orders
fields.description: If ticked, the Pending SO list above changes this engine's behaviour. If not, the SO list is ignored for this engine.

fields.label: Reads Work Orders
fields.description: If ticked, the Pending WO list above changes this engine's behaviour. If not, it is ignored.

fields.label: Reads Purchase Orders
fields.description: If ticked, the Pending PO list above changes this engine's behaviour. If not, it is ignored.

fields.label: Creates Purchase MRs
fields.description: If ticked, this engine creates Purchase Material Requests for purchase-mode items and nets pending Purchase MRs out of the shortage (the Pending Purchase MR list above applies). If not, the MR list is ignored.
